reasoning: Based on independent searches and reviewing the Wikipedia list of infectious diseases as of October 2025, there is no vaccine currently developed or even in known clinical trials for human bocavirus. The article from MedicineNet explicitly states "there is no vaccine in development for human use" as the virus typically causes mild respiratory symptoms matching the common cold, predominantly in children. Vaccine development and approval requires years of clinical trials. Given that there are no advanced candidates and the resolution dates are extremely short term (within 3 months), it is virtually impossible that a vaccine will be developed and added to the Wikipedia table in this timeframe. The only pathway for a "YES" resolution would be an uncorrected error or vandalism on the Wikipedia page exactly on the resolution date. Therefore, I assign the lowest possible probabilities according to the allowed ranges, slightly increasing for the furthest date to account for any unforeseen edge cases or Wikipedia editing anomalies.
